steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Group the $x$ and $y$ terms, then complete each square:
$$
x^2-6x+y^2-4y-51=0
$$
$$
(x^2-6x+9)+(y^2-4y+4)=51+9+4
$$

steps.title: Find the circle radius
steps.content:
The equation becomes
$$
(x-3)^2+(y-2)^2=64
$$
So the radius is
$$
r=\sqrt{64}=8
$$

steps.title: Use the square diameter
steps.content: A circle inscribed in a square has diameter equal to the square's side length. Thus $$ s=2r=16 $$ $$ 4s=4(16)=64 $$ The answer is C.
